name: Toyota Supra Mk4 (A80)
engine: 3.0L twin-turbo I6 (2JZ-GTE, sequential turbos; NA cars use the 2JZ-GE)
torque: 315 lb-ft (US); JDM rated 276 hp under the gentleman's agreement
reliability: 9/10 - the 2JZ-GTE is one of the most overbuilt engines ever sold: closed-deck iron block and forged crank that shrug off 600-800 whp on stock internals once fueling and turbos are sorted. The car is robust; the real risks are age and abuse, not the engineering.
cost_to_own: Insurance: collector/agreed-value territory · Mechanicals are stout but parts are increasingly scarce and pricey, and clean unmodified examples have appreciated hard (often $80k-$150k+).
popular_mods: Boost + fueling (injectors, fuel pump, standalone ECU) - big gains on the stock 2JZ-GTE bottom end
Single-turbo conversion - the classic path past the sequential setup to 600+ whp
Forged internals only when chasing very high power; stock bottom end handles a lot first
Coilovers, sway bars, big brake kit to match the added power
Upgraded clutch/transmission to hold torque on built cars

generations.A40/A60 (1978-1986): Celica Supra - the inline-six GT origins of the nameplate.
generations.A80 (1993-2002): This car. Legendary 2JZ-GTE twin-turbo (320 hp US / 276 hp JDM), 1,000 hp+ capable on built fueling. US-market turbo sold 1993-1998; the NA 2JZ-GE made 220 hp. The car that built the Supra legend.
generations.A90/A91 (2020-present): Revived after 17 years on BMW B58 power (335->382 hp). Co-developed with BMW, built by Magna Steyr.

common_issues: Targa-top seals harden with age - wind noise, rattles, and water leaks
Rust on neglected cars (rear hatch, wheel arches, battery tray)
Many were heavily modified and hard-driven - history and a compression/leakdown check matter more than the odometer
Aging factory electronics, gauges, and R12 air conditioning
maintenance_tips: Buy on documented condition and service history, not just mileage - so many were modified
Compression and leakdown test before purchase; the 2JZ is tough but abuse still shows
Stay on top of targa seals and treat any rust early
Don't over-boost the stock sequential turbos - upgrade fueling before chasing power
Use quality fluids on schedule; overbuilt doesn't mean maintenance-free
